parsed_review.summary: CP00-212 (RP06.P04.M03.S09-RP06.P04.M03.S18, risk class A, 10 units) is a descriptor-only DMS-core UI primary slice tail pack. Read-only inspection confirms the production_ready posture is correct and supported: the manifest sets production_ready=false / status=implemented_pending_review, explicitly does not claim enterprise trust from local validation, and requires human final approval. Implementation is internally consistent across registry bindings (DMS_CORE_CP212_PACK_BINDING / _REQUIREMENTS / _NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION), service builders, validators, tests, the committed contract projection, the closeout plan, the next-pack queue, and the README. The deliverable distribution {security_audit:2, implementation:3, ui:2, fixture:1, test:1, hermes_evidence:1}=10 matches the plan and the validator requirements. All 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via packages/dms/src/index.js wildcard exports (explaining why index.js is unmodified). No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime/no-leak boundaries are enforced via frozen attestation objects and asserted by both the test suite (packages/dms/test/model.test.js) and the contract validation script (scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs); a grep for fs/http/fetch/child_process/db/process.env found no actual IO, network, persistence, or runtime operations in the changed source. Claude is bound to read-only reviewer and NOT final approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, allowed_tools=Read/Grep/Glob). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_ui_primary_slice_tail_no_write); no product-runtime Hermes execution. The committed contract's ui_primary_slice_tail_descriptor matches the generated descriptor (not stale). Review-evidence hardening holds: the readiness auth preflight returned a valid non-empty JSON result (not 0-byte, not malformed, not fenced/tool-shaped) and no invalid review attempts are recorded. The required post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this pre-review stage since they are produced from this review and the downstream adjudication/inspection steps. Only one trivial cosmetic finding (P3) was identified in the generated changed-file-scope.json; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Pack and goal closeout are not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists two pack files as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-212/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 165-168, correct) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 173-174). The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 524-537) does NOT list these two paths, so the scope artifact is internally inconsistent with the manifest. This is a cosmetic quirk of how the generated scope file aggregates git status; both paths are genuinely part of the CP00-212 pack (the pack evidence directory and the session handoff doc). It does not affect the production_ready determination, the descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries, the Claude read-only authority boundary, or any validation gate.
findings.recommendation: Optionally adjust the scope-generation tooling (scripts/run-closeout-pack-claude-review.mjs or its scope builder) so a path classified as untracked_implementation_files is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, keeping the scope artifact consistent with the manifest. Non-blocking; no pack content change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational. Does not block CP00-212 pack closeout or goal closeout; safe to defer to a tooling cleanup.
